reasoning: The 'unsafe' verdict was based solely on torrent_hashes_update.sh using a hardcoded SSH key, but this script isn't executed during makepkg. All build/install hooks are safe, and the flagged script is a maintainer-only tool irrelevant to end-user installation.
disagreement_analysis: Qwen overreacted to a non-executed maintainer script while DeepSeek correctly contextualized it.
coverage_issues: Both models missed reviewing .install hooks if present
false_positives: torrent_hashes_update.sh (qwen): Maintainer script not executed during build/install
learnings: Improve threat model emphasis: Prioritize files executed during makepkg (PKGBUILD funcs, .install scripts)
Clarify maintainer/CI scripts are low-risk unless invoked by build
Enhance file prioritization: PKGBUILD > .install > build hooks > other scripts
